sentence_reviews.text: Formal agent verification checks plans, policies, tool sequences, or generated programs against state, temporal, safety, and authorization specifications.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey supports formal specifications and verification; AIMA supports planning/state/action formalisms; structured-output survey supports constrained/typed generation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible synthesis across sources; authorization/tool-sequence specifics are not directly established by one source.

sentence_reviews.text: It adds a machine-checkable control layer between an agent’s proposal and an external side effect.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods provide machine-checkable specification satisfaction; structured outputs can constrain generated actions before execution.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible synthesis for agent safety, not directly verified.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods survey covers temporal/safety specifications; AIMA covers planning formalisms.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as a standard formal-planning verification example.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Open-world observations and underspecified environments make complete verification difficult, so runtime monitoring remains necessary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey discusses specification/model limitations and scalability; NIST supports ongoing monitoring and risk management.
sentence_reviews.notes: Well supported as synthesis; runtime monitoring necessity is governance reasoning.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Formal verification of agents & plans has an `evaluates_or_constrains` dependency on Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations because Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations is modeled as evaluation, constraint, audit, or governance support.
